gate_claim: Phase A retires the fast/full paths completely and correctly with zero adaptive regression: every fast/full script, test, command, skill and forge port is removed; installed_paths/WORKFLOW_PATHS/resolveInstalledPaths machinery is gone with claim.js default flipped to adaptive and cmdFinalize plan-absent collapsed to a typed adaptive_plan_missing refusal in the SAME change; every kept caller of the removed symbols is updated; the next routing surfaces are regenerated without fast/full; the validator/test/install/package.json assertion surface reflects the retired state; opencode/kimi are re-synced; classifier.js and validation-runner.js are untouched; and all four edition chains plus the opencode and kimi suites are green

## Finding digest provenance (all sha256, computed in the worktree)

- precondition_digest (F1/F2/F3) = sha256(kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/n1-recon.md). expected_digest (F1/F2/F3) = sha256 of the verbatim gate_claim string (matches the review context claim_digest byte-exact).
- Anchor rebinding note: the anchor index admits only WRITE-SET producers (adaptive-node.js:1520), so F1 anchors to n9-validators.md (its evidence quotes the three fixture files + refusal JSON) and F3 anchors to n6-routing.md (the finalize-wiring producer; its 3-of-6 surface completion exposes the residual packs); n1-recon.md (read-only, empty write set) remains the precondition_digest binding on all three.
- F1 input = sha256(cat scripts/test-bundle-finalize.js plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-sinks.js plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-sinks.js); F1 observed = sha256 of the exact adaptive_plan_missing refusal JSON those fixtures emit; F1 anchor producer = n1-recon.md digest (unowned files; the manifest scoped them).
- F2 input = sha256(cat scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js); F2 observed = sha256 of the exact validator failure string (CLAUDE.md must document compact durable state contract; missing: fast-summary.md); F2 anchor producer = sha256(kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/n9-validators.md).
- F3 input = sha256(cat of the three plugins/*/skills/kaola-workflow-finalize/SKILL.md in canonical/gitlab/gitea order); F3 observed = sha256 of the grep -n full-advance output over those three files; F3 anchor producer = n1-recon.md digest (unowned files; GAP-1 row scoped them).

## Review narrative

Process: read the review context (discovery phase, no prior findings, no validation_obligations), the n1 retirement manifest, and all nine upstream evidence files n2–n10; then independently verified the full accumulated diff vs claim root base 33a1ca57 (HEAD == base; 120 changed tracked paths = 56 staged deletions + 64 modifications + 1 new file docs/decisions/D-725-01.md) and ran every suite/chain myself.

Per-point verification against the 10-point task direction:

1. Deletions — PASS. Exactly 56 `D` entries, breaking down 4+9+4+18+18+3 exactly as the manifest; `git ls-files` grep for any surviving fast/full path script/test/command/skill/forge-port returns zero.
2. Symbol contract — PASS. `WORKFLOW_PATHS = Object.freeze(['adaptive'])` in all four adaptive-schema copies (line 24, 4-way byte-identical per script-sync); `resolveInstalledPaths`/`INSTALLED_PATHS_FIELD` survive only as three retirement comments (validate-workflow-contracts.js:833 x2, simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:11) — no kept caller calls the removed symbols anywhere in the four trees.
3. Trap 4 — PASS. Scaffold default `data.workflow_path || adaptiveSchema.ADAPTIVE_PATH` at claim.js canonical/codex :790 and gitlab/gitea :677; cmdFinalize plan-absent collapse to `finalize_gate_unverified`/`adaptive_plan_missing` present in all four editions (canonical/codex :2868, gitlab/gitea :2638); canonical==codex byte-identical; zero full-advance/isFast/'full' residue in claim.js x4. `install.sh --with-fast` exits 2 Unknown-argument (n5's AC2 regression ran green inside my claude-chain run); the claim-surface unknown_flag assertion lives in test-claim-hardening.js (green, 520 assertions).
4. Traps 1/2/5 — PASS. `git diff --stat` vs base is EMPTY for classifier.js x4, validation-runner.js, templates/routing/slots.js, and templates/routing/required-blocks.js. `escalated_to_full` preserved (adaptive-node 32 occurrences, repair-state 2); unrelated "full"/"fast-forward" vocabulary intact in the skeleton/finalize commands; stale `installed_paths` is tolerated on read (classifier's tolerant default untouched) and never written (install.sh `config.pop("installed_paths", None)` at :806; codex installer seedKaolaConfig arity 1).
5. Routing — PASS. `generate-routing-surfaces.js --check` green ("all 12 surfaces byte-match the skeleton" — machine-regenerated, not hand-edited); no plan-run surface appears in the diff (byte-unchanged); the three finalize COMMAND copies contain zero full-advance/`workflow_path: fast`/`workflow_path: full` hits. The three finalize SKILL packs do — that is F3.
6. Assertion surface + six walkthroughs — PARTIAL. package.json dropped the 4 dead tests and the stale 6-phase description; the codex walkthrough passes fully standalone (EXIT 0); the canonical walkthrough reds ONLY at testContractValidatorOfflineSkip (F2's validator, shelled against the live tree); the gitlab/gitea and forge-codex walkthroughs red ONLY where their run() chains shell the unowned sinks tests (F1). The validators themselves are retired correctly except the F2 CLAUDE.md pin.
7. opencode/kimi — PASS. Re-synced (sync --check parity per n7) and both suites green under my own runs: 396 and 440 assertions, exit 0.
8. Cross-edition parity — PASS. `edition-sync.js --check` green (12 ports, 22 COMMON_SCRIPTS, 28 byte groups); `validate-script-sync.js` green (22/28/5 with the retired entries dropped); claim canonical==codex byte-identical; gitlab/gitea claim carry the mirrored trap-4 hunks.
9. Four chains + additive suites — FAIL. Run sequentially, all four chains exit 1: claude red at test-bundle-finalize.js (F1) AND validate-workflow-contracts.js + walkthrough (F2) — I additionally ran every claude step behind the short-circuit: test-sink-merge (105), test-parallel, test-edition-sync (46), test-release-surface-drift (9), test-release (244), test-gap-sweep (85), test-ledger-compare (18), test-validation-allowband (32), test-route-reachability (2277), test-agent-profile-parity (461), test-bash-block-guards, test-run-chains (163), generate/test-generate-routing-surfaces all green, so F1+F2 are the complete claude frontier; codex red at validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js (F2) with its walkthrough green standalone; gitlab red at test-gitlab-sinks.js:826 (F1); gitea red at test-gitea-sinks.js:791 (F1). opencode/kimi suites green.
10. Docs — PASS. CLAUDE.md 142 lines (<200); D-725-01.md filed with correct Supersedes (D-538-01/D-543-01 byte-untouched per git status).

Scope audit: the 64 modified files map exactly onto the declared n3–n10 write sets (12+7+8+11+2+6+9+9) with zero strays.

Non-blocking documented residuals (deliberate plan-scope deferrals, disclosed in n1 SS2/SS5, n6's discovered-gap note, and n10's CHANGELOG + D-725-01 — chain-safe, no deleted-script shell, not gate_claim obligations this phase): GAP-2 (workflow-init command/SKILL x6 stale --with-fast/--with-full/installed_paths prose), GAP-3 (install-opencode.sh/install-kimi.sh still parse the retired flags and write installed_paths — additive editions, own suites green), GAP-4 (.env.example prose), and required-blocks.js's dormant fn-fast-compliance-backstop manifest entry that force-keeps the reframed legacy PIN paragraph on the 6 finalize surfaces. None admitted as blockers.

Gate verdict: changes_requested (domain_outcome: fail). All three blockers are anchored OUTSIDE the tail producer's write set (F1/F3 in files no node owns; F2 in n9-owned validators upstream of n10), so none is an n10 reopen-in-place candidate — this is the claim-preserving replan path. The three F1 files share one root cause and one already-twice-proven fix (seedAdaptiveFinalizeFixture migration); F2 is a three-line pin removal across the three validators; F3 mirrors n6's finalize-command edit onto the three SKILL packs, closing the 6-surface propagation.

review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: Phase A's core surgery is faithful — the 56 deletions are exact, the symbol contract and trap-4 collapse hold across all four editions, the traps are respected, routing is cleanly regenerated, and opencode/kimi plus parity checks are green — but the gate claim's four-chain-green condition is factually unmet: all four edition chains are red on three unowned fast-marker finalize fixtures, a newly exposed n9 validator pin on retired CLAUDE.md vocabulary, and the three unowned Codex finalize SKILL packs still shelling the deleted full-advance script, so the verdict is changes_requested pending a claim-preserving replan that assigns those three orphaned repair surfaces.
